description: Run a CPU-reference correctness harness against the kernel output before trusting any benchmark number.
body: Use when a GPU kernel has been edited and you need ground truth on whether it still computes the right result. Procedure: build a small deterministic input, compute the expected output on the CPU (or a known-good reference kernel), run the GPU kernel, and compare element-wise within an explicit floating-point tolerance. Report the max absolute and relative error, the tolerance used, and the input shape. Do not use a benchmark pass, a clean compile, or visual inspection as evidence of correctness; only a reference comparison is evidence. This skill exposes capability only and must not run device code without runtime permission. Risk boundary: if the comparison fails or tolerance is unclear, stop and surface the numerical drift rather than continuing to optimize.
